full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Identifying the main baseline the paper compares against
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: In the FGM paper (Tan et al., ICML 2010), which method do they mainly compare to — is it SVM-RFE or something else? I want to name it correctly for Task 1.3.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: It said SVM-RFE is the main baseline. I re-read Section 1 and Section 3 and confirmed they compare to SVM-RFE and also report SVM with all features. I wrote the baseline name and the limitation in my own words.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Section 1 and Table 3 in the paper explicitly compare FGM to SVM-RFE. I cited that in my notebook.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Thinking of a scenario where FGM might not beat the baseline
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Task 1.3 asks for one condition where the paper's method might not outperform the baseline — it can be my own inference. For FGM vs SVM-RFE, when would FGM not do better?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: It suggested things like when the data has strong feature interactions or when B is too small. I thought about it and wrote that when the true signal is spread over many interacting features, the c_j^2 ranking might miss them and SVM-RFE could do better — I linked this to my Task 1.2 assumption about marginal importance.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: This matches the violation scenario I already had in Task 1.2. I did not copy the LLM's wording; I stated the condition in one sentence in the notebook.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Task 1.3. I used the LLM to confirm the baseline name and to brainstorm one failure condition; I wrote all four parts of Task 1.3 (baseline, limitation, improvement, condition) myself after checking the paper.
